items.title: Darius Garland says he has his "joy" back after leaving the Cavs: "Coming to a system where I could actually be me, have the ball in my hands the majority of the game, have control over the pace of the game...it was really good. It was fun. I got my joy back"
items.author: marriagethrowaway36

items.body_excerpt: On this episode of Ball in the Family, hosts Lonzo Ball, LiAngelo Ball, Anthony Salazar, and Darren Moore welcome NBA All-Star Darius Garland for a wide-ranging conversation packed with NBA playoff talk, locker room stories, hot takes, and nonstop laughs. Garland reunites with…

items.title: [Fedor] The Cavaliers contemplated a Darius Garland for Jrue Holiday swap before ultimately trading for James Harden. They ultimately determined Holiday wasn’t enough of a “reliable offensive engine.”
items.author: YujiDomainExpansion
items.body_excerpt: Source: https://www.cleveland.com/cavs/2026/06/will-cavs-consider-significant-changes-this-summer-what-moves-are-possible-chris-fedor.html If it wasn’t Harden, it might’ve been someone else. The Cavs wondered about Boston’s appetite for moving Derrick White — a non-starter given…

items.title: Curious how many would do the Garland + a 2nd round pick for Harden deal that the Cavs did at the deadline.
items.author: Austin Krell
items.body_excerpt:
Curious how many would do the Garland + a 2nd round pick for Harden deal that the Cavs did at the deadline.

It’s viewed as “falling for the Harden act” but Garland had durability issues all season and a restrictive contract. Don’t think it was awful business, regardless of Gansey’s role in it.

items.title: When the Cavaliers tell you how much better they are with James Harden, it means they believe they couldn’t win with Darius Garland. What about the rest of the original core four? And the coach? We’re about to find out www.nytimes.com/athletic/730...
